data.title: Olo Non-GAAP EPS of $0.08 beats by $0.02, revenue of $80.7M beats by $3.26M
data.description:
• Olo press release (NYSE:OLO): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.08 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $80.7M (+21.3% Y/Y) beats by $3.26M.
  • Total platform revenue increased 20% year-over-year to $79.2 million.
  • Average revenue per unit increased 12% year-over-year to approximately $911.
  • Dollar-based net revenue retention (NRR) was 111%.
  • Ending active locations increased 8% year-over year to approximately 88,000, up approximately 2,000 from the quarter ended December 31, 2024.

  • As of May 8, 2025, Olo is issuing the following outlook:

    For the second quarter of 2025, Olo expects to report:

    • Revenue in the range of $82.0 million to $82.5 million; and
    • Non-GAAP operating income in the range of $11.5 million to $11.8 million.

  • For fiscal year 2025, Olo expects to report:

    • Revenue in the range of $338.5 million to $340.0 million; and
    • Non-GAAP operating income in the range of $48.6 million to $49.8 million.

data.title: Suzano S.A. reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Suzano S.A. press release (NYSE:SUZ): Q1 net revenue of R$11.6 billion, up 22% on the same quarter last year.
  • Adjusted EBITDA totaled R$4.9 billion, a 7% increase over 1Q24.
  • Net profit totaled R$6.3 billion due to the accounting impact of U.S. dollar denominated debt and hedging operations translating into Brazilian Real.

data.title: Clean energy fuels outlines growth in RNG adoption and X15N opportunities for 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Clean Energy Fuels Corp. (CLNE) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Andrew Littlefair highlighted that Clean Energy Fuels achieved solid results in Q1, selling 51 million gallons of renewable natural gas (RNG), generating $104 million in revenue, and $17 million in adjusted EBITDA. RNG sales volumes were impacted by lower supply from third-party producers but are expected to recover.
  • The company ended the quarter with $227 million in cash, an increase of $9 million from the start of the year. Management emphasized this strong position supports their growth initiatives.
  • Littlefair noted that tariffs have had minimal direct impact on the business as their operations are concentrated in the U.S. and Canada. However, there is concern about potential indirect effects on the heavy-duty trucking sector.
  • CFO Robert Vreeland explained that the planned removal of LNG station equipment from various Pilot Flying J sites is underway as part of a strategic shift, with most of the related accelerated depreciation recorded in Q1.
  • Vreeland clarified that a $115 million non-cash charge, including a goodwill write-off, impacted the GAAP loss for the quarter.

Outlook

  • Management maintained the full-year financial outlook and CapEx guidance provided in the previous quarter, with confidence in policy outcomes potentially providing upside.
  • Littlefair addressed the introduction of the Cummins X15N engine, emphasizing its potential for adoption in the heavy-duty trucking sector, though initial uptake is anticipated to be gradual.
  • RNG upstream projects are progressing, with six projects in operation and two more expected online by year-end.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 was $17.1 million, marking an improvement from $12.8 million in the same quarter last year.
  • The company emphasized a steady demand from fleet customers, with growth in fuel volumes and maintenance services contributing positively.
  • The absence of the Alternative Fuel Tax Credit (AFTC) in Q1, which contributed $5.4 million in revenue in the prior year, was noted as a key factor in revenue comparison.
  • Clean Energy Fuels continues to optimize RNG production and distribution, with plans to expand existing customer relationships and projects.

Q&A

  • Dushyant Ailani, Jefferies: Questioned the impact of tariffs and the company's financial outlook. CEO Littlefair indicated optimism for market stabilization and emphasized the compelling economics of RNG.
  • Eric Stine, Craig Hallum: Inquired about adoption challenges for the Cummins X15N engine. Littlefair acknowledged early challenges with incremental costs but expressed confidence in competitive pricing improvements driving wider adoption.
  • Rob Brown, Lake Street Capital Markets: Asked about RNG facilities' contribution timelines. Littlefair shared that new RNG projects are expected to ramp up by 2026, contributing positively to EBITDA.
  • Matthew Blair, TPH: Sought clarity on Q1 performance drivers. CFO Vreeland attributed it to a strong core fueling business and effective cost controls.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts conveyed cautious optimism, highlighting challenges in RNG production and adoption of the X15N engine.
  • Management showed confidence in the company's strategic positioning, emphasizing growth potential despite external uncertainties.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, there was a stronger focus on addressing operational and regulatory challenges, reflecting a slightly defensive tone.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • RNG sales volumes decreased compared to Q4 2024, attributed to supply constraints from third-party producers.
  • Adjusted EBITDA improved on a year-over-year basis, indicating operational efficiency gains.
  • Management's tone shifted toward cautious optimism, with a focus on navigating regulatory and market challenges.
  • Analysts maintained a consistent focus on the adoption of the X15N engine and its implications for long-term growth.

Risks and Concerns

  • Supply constraints from third-party RNG producers, impacted by weather and operational issues, were noted as a challenge.
  • Regulatory uncertainties, including the status of the AFTC and 45Z Clean Fuel Production credit, remain a concern for long-term financial planning.
  • Management highlighted indirect risks from tariffs affecting the heavy-duty trucking sector's purchasing decisions.

Final Takeaway

Clean Energy Fuels Corp. reported a strong start to 2025, with robust adjusted EBITDA growth and steady demand for RNG despite supply constraints. The company remains focused on expanding RNG production and adoption of the Cummins X15N engine, which is expected to drive long-term growth. While challenges such as regulatory uncertainties and incremental costs for new technologies persist, management's strategic initiatives and financial stability position the company to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the clean energy sector.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Altice USA targets $3.4B adjusted EBITDA for 2025 with growth in fiber and mobile segments
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Altice USA, Inc. (ATUS) Q1 2025

Management View

  • Dennis Mathew, Chairman and CEO, highlighted that Altice USA made progress in stabilizing performance with improvements in customer and network experience. Notable achievements include reaching over 600,000 fiber customers and surpassing 500,000 mobile lines. Fiber penetration hit 20%, with a goal of 30% by year-end 2026.
  • The company is targeting $3.4 billion in adjusted EBITDA for 2025, emphasizing operational efficiency and optimizing capital structure. Recent actions include an agreement to sell certain tower assets for $60 million and another for the i24NEWS business.
  • CFO Marc Sirota emphasized the company's adjusted EBITDA target and projected full-year 2025 revenue between $8.6 billion and $8.7 billion. Measures such as programming cost savings and moderated operating expenses are expected to support these targets.

Outlook

  • Management reiterated its full-year 2025 adjusted EBITDA guidance of approximately $3.4 billion and revenue projection of $8.6 billion to $8.7 billion. The company expects subscriber stabilization, improved ARPU trends, and expanded adoption of value-added services to drive these results.
  • Fiber penetration is projected to reach 30% by the end of 2026, supported by investments in network upgrades and targeted market strategies.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 total revenue was approximately $2.2 billion, reflecting a year-over-year decline of 4.4%. Adjusted EBITDA stood at $799 million, down 5.6% year-over-year. Gross margin improved to 68.8%, partly due to programming cost savings.
  • Residential ARPU decreased by 1.3% to $133.93, affected by customer credits and programming interruptions. Broadband ARPU increased by 2.4% to $75.31, driven by rate actions and upgrades.
  • Free cash flow for Q1 2025 was negative $169 million, attributed to higher cash interest payments and other financial adjustments.

Q&A

  • Michael Rollins, Citi: Asked about broadband competition and customer behavior trends. CEO Dennis Mathew highlighted the intense competitive landscape, including fiber and fixed wireless challenges. He mentioned new income-constrained product launches aimed at addressing economic pressures for targeted customer segments.
  • Jonathan Chaplin, New Street Research: Queried about the status of discussions with bondholders. CFO Marc Sirota confirmed that negotiations had concluded without an agreement and reiterated the company’s focus on managing debt maturities.
  • Craig Moffett, MoffettNathanson: Asked about pricing competitiveness. CEO Mathew explained improvements in pricing strategies through AI and hyper-localized approaches, allowing the company to balance competitiveness and ARPU growth.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns about broadband competition, subscriber trends, and capital structure. Their tone was cautious but demonstrated interest in the company's strategic responses.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, especially regarding operational improvements and targeted strategies to stabilize business performance. Confidence phrases included "we are excited" and "we remain disciplined."

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance remained consistent with previous quarter targets, including the $3.4 billion adjusted EBITDA projection for 2025. Revenue guidance adjusted slightly downward to $8.6 billion-$8.7 billion.
  • Strategic focus shifted toward monetizing non-core assets and expanding income-constrained and hyper-localized offerings.
  • Analyst sentiment remained cautious, with focus on competitive dynamics and financial sustainability.

Risks and Concerns

  • Competitive pressures from fiber overbuilders and fixed wireless providers were highlighted.
  • Inflation and economic pressures impacting income-constrained customers were noted, with management introducing targeted offerings to mitigate challenges.
  • Debt maturities beyond 2027 present a medium-term concern, with management exploring various options for capital structure optimization.

Final Takeaway

Altice USA is making strategic strides to stabilize performance with a focus on fiber and mobile growth, operational efficiency, and targeted market strategies. The company reaffirmed its adjusted EBITDA target of $3.4 billion for 2025 while navigating competitive pressures and economic challenges. Management's focus on cost control and monetization of non-core assets aims to strengthen financial flexibility and sustain long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Trinseo revises guidance amid strategic shifts and tariff impacts
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Trinseo (TSE) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Frank Bozich highlighted that Q1 core business results aligned with expectations, with adjusted EBITDA reaching $65 million, an improvement driven by restructuring actions, better business mix, and a polycarbonate licensing agreement.
  • The licensing agreement with Deepak Chem Tech contributed $26 million in income this quarter and is estimated to be worth $52 million in total, indicating progress in Trinseo's strategic partnership in India.
  • Bozich indicated that the company is focusing on geographic expansion and sustainable product solutions, noting a 33% rise in recycled content products and a 43% increase in consumer electronic applications. Strength in PMMA resin volumes in Asia and case volumes also provided growth opportunities.
  • CFO David Stasse reported negative $119 million in free cash flow for Q1, largely due to seasonal working capital outflows and refinancing-related expenses. Free cash flow is expected to be breakeven in Q2 and turn positive in the latter half of 2025.
  • Stasse also noted segment performance, with Latex Binders maintaining stability despite lower volumes in China, and Polymer Solutions achieving higher adjusted EBITDA year-over-year due to licensing income and restructuring benefits.

Outlook

  • Trinseo withdrew its full-year 2025 guidance due to rising economic and geopolitical uncertainties. The company now only provides guidance for Q2, expecting adjusted EBITDA to range between $55 million and $70 million.
  • Management acknowledged that tariff uncertainties are impacting demand, particularly in China and the automotive sector. However, they anticipate positive regional pricing and demand effects in areas where Trinseo operates as a local producer.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 was $65 million, up $20 million year-over-year.
  • Segment-specific highlights included a 50% year-over-year increase in specialty and sustainable PMMA product volumes in China and improved Latex Binders margins due to a favorable product mix.
  • Licensing income of $26 million from the Deepak Chem Tech agreement significantly bolstered results in the quarter.
  • Free cash flow was negative at $119 million, impacted by $25 million in refinancing costs and typical seasonal working capital outflows.

Q&A

  • Hassan Ahmed, Alembic Global Partners, asked about the sustainability of Q1 volume growth amid tariff uncertainties. Bozich assured that Q1 demand continued into Q2 without signs of pre-buying or distortion.
  • Ahmed followed up on cash flow guidance for Q2. Stasse expressed confidence due to visibility into May results and inventory management strategies, noting the collection of $21 million from the Deepak licensing deal in April.
  • Matthew Blair, TPH, inquired about AmSty performance and the sale process. Bozich reiterated confidence in monetizing AmSty at an optimal valuation environment, while Stasse highlighted a $10 million timing impact on Q1 results.
  • Blair also asked about battery binder opportunities. Bozich emphasized the company's growth in supplying anode binders for lithium-ion batteries, capitalizing on Trinseo’s global footprint and technology advantages.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed mixed sentiment, with concerns about tariff impacts and cash flow sustainability, balanced by optimism around licensing income and specialty product growth.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during prepared remarks but showed caution regarding geopolitical uncertainties and near-term demand risks. "We are confident in our ability to navigate these uncertain business conditions," stated Bozich.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was slightly more cautious, reflecting the withdrawal of full-year guidance.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 adjusted EBITDA of $65 million marked an improvement from Q4, largely due to the $26 million licensing income and seasonal factors.
  • The company withdrew the full-year guidance provided in Q4, citing increased unpredictability.
  • Management emphasized growth in specialty applications, while analysts focused more on tariff-related risks and cash flow projections.
  • Analysts' tone became more pressing this quarter, particularly regarding AmSty performance and cash flow clarity.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tariff uncertainties were highlighted as a significant risk, with potential impacts on demand in China and the automotive sector.
  • Management acknowledged the challenge of navigating volatile energy costs, particularly in Europe, although they noted reduced exposure due to prior asset closures.
  • Analysts raised concerns about cash flow sustainability and the timing of the AmSty sale process.

Final Takeaway

Trinseo's Q1 2025 results reflected a mix of strategic progress and external challenges. While licensing income and growth in specialty products demonstrated the company's transformation strategy, uncertainties around tariffs and cash flow remain key concerns. Management's focus on sustainable solutions and geographic expansion, particularly in India and China, positions the company for long-term growth despite near-term headwinds.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
5:55 AM Fed's Barr Speech 
 
Federal Reserve Board Governor Michael Barr will speak on"Artificial Intelligence and the Labor Market" before the Reykjavik Economic Conference organized by the Central Bank of Iceland and the Center for International Macroeconomics at Northwestern University.
 
6:15 AM Fed's Williams Speech 
 
New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will speak before the Reykjavik Economic Conference organized by the Central Bank of Iceland and the Center for International Macroeconomics at Northwestern University.
 
6:45 AM Fed's Kugler Speech 
 
Federal Reserve Board Governor Adriana Kugler will speak on"Maximum Employment" before the Reykjavik Economic Conference organized by the Central Bank of Iceland and the Center for International Macroeconomics at Northwestern University.
 
8:30 AM Fed's Barkin Speech 
 
Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin will participate in a fireside chat before the Loudon County Chamber of Commerce.
 
10:00 AM Fed's Goolsbee Speech 
 
Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee will give welcome and opening remarks before the"Fed Listens: Perspectives from the Midwest" event.
 
11:30 AM Fed's Williams Speech 
 
New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams will speak on"Taylor Rules in Policy" before the Hoover Monetary Policy Conference.
 
11:30 AM Fed's Waller Speech 
 
Federal Reserve Board Governor Christopher Waller will speak on"Monetary Policy Research". 
 
1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count 
 
The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs.
 
7:45 PM Fed's Hammack Speech 
 
Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack will speak on monetary policy before the Hoover Monetary Policy Conference.
 
7:45 PM Hoover Monetary Policy Conference 
 
Federal Reserve Board Governor Lisa Cook, Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland President Beth Hammack, Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis President Alberto Musalem will participate in monetary policy panel before the Hoover Monetary Policy Conference.
